decision_target: Choose the managed Claude session transport and lifecycle
source_of_truth: Installed Claude Code 2.1.207 help and live stream-json probe
read_method: Replay the help read and one tools-disabled stream-json turn from the pumped-fn repository
max_age: 7 days or any Claude CLI version change
evidence_refs_or_hashes: command:printf stream-json user message | claude -p --verbose --input-format stream-json --output-format stream-json --tools '' --permission-mode dontAsk --no-session-persistence
replay_command_or_checker: printf '%s\n' '{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"Reply with exactly: PUMPED_SESSION_OK"}}' | claude -p --verbose --input-format stream-json --output-format stream-json --tools '' --permission-mode dontAsk --no-session-persistence
questions_answered: A pumped-owned Claude process can exchange structured input and lifecycle/result events without MCP or a new IPC protocol
The parent can explicitly set roots, tools, permissions, session identity, and persistence policy
questions_unanswered: Multi-turn process behavior and interrupt acknowledgement need a focused lifecycle probe
The final shared contract must not expose Claude-specific hook or rate-limit shapes
risk_or_anti_goal_implications: Reduces AG-1 risk because process IO can remain behind declared flows and a scope-owned resource
Reduces AG-3 risk because roots and permissions are explicit CLI inputs
Keeps AG-5 held because no MCP or tool projection is required for session control
